ambient: A quiet decade under the Ascension Circle: the med-bays ran through the night shifts, and more of the crew each year wore the small silver ports of the augmented at their temples.
The ship ran clean and cool and a little inhuman on the Circle's watch — schedules optimized, sentiment logged and filed, the founders' warmth traded for a precision they would not have recognized.
name: The Ascension Circle
description: Transhumanist zealots who read the voyage as a chrysalis — flesh is a first draft, to be rewritten in transit. They welcome every augmentation the ship can bear.

recruit_boon.flavor: The Ascension Circle came aboard, and with them a body of knowledge the ship's medics had only read about — augmentation, gene-work, the long study of remaking the flesh for the dark.

ambient: Under the Steel Covenant the ship never quite fell silent — somewhere a shop was always running, a weld always cooling, the makers unable to leave a thing unmended or unimproved.
A maker's peace: the corridors smelled of hot metal and machine oil, and every fixture the crew touched had been rebuilt at least once, better than the founders drew it.
name: The Steel Covenant
description: Machinists and engineers who trust tools over doctrine. If a thing can be built, maintained, and improved, the Covenant will build it — and argue the theology later.

recruit_boon.flavor: The Steel Covenant came aboard and walked straight into the engineering bay, and by the end of the shift it ran a shade truer than it had in a generation. The makers brought their craft with them.

ambient: The Accord's quiet was a lawful one — disputes filed and heard and settled, the ship's long grievances worn smooth by patient arbitration, everything in its docket and its place.
Under the Meridian Accord the decks ran to procedure; even the silences seemed scheduled, and the crew took a dry comfort in a ship where everything had a rule, and the rule was usually followed.
name: The Meridian Accord
description: Pragmatic administrators who hold the middle line, weighing each innovation against each tradition and committing wholly to neither.

recruit_boon.flavor: The Meridian Accord came aboard and set up their courts before their quarters, and the ship's uneasy peace acquired, overnight, a body of law and the patient hands to keep it.

ambient: A Hearth decade: the long tables stayed set, the corridors smelled of cooking and close company, and the ship felt less like a machine crossing the dark than a very large family that happened to live in one.
Under the Hearth Union the ship kept its festivals and its griefs together, out loud, at shared tables — a warmth the founders would have known and been glad the voyage still carried.
name: The Hearth Union
description: Keepers of home and hearth who prize continuity — the shared meal, the remembered name — over the cold promise of the new.

recruit_boon.flavor: The Hearth Union came aboard and, within a week, there was a common table on every deck that had gone without one. The ship felt, for the first time in a long while, like somewhere people lived rather than travelled.

ambient: The Verdant Kin's quiet grew things: the grow-decks ran lush past all need, green crept into corridors never meant for it, and the ship breathed a little easier for a people who could not stop tending living things.
A garden peace under the Kin — the air sweet with growth, the reclaimers fat and idle, the ship greener each year than the founders had dared hope a sealed hull could stay.
name: The Verdant Kin
description: Soil-tenders who measure worth in living things. They distrust the sealed machine and keep the ship's green heart beating against the dark.

recruit_boon.flavor: The Verdant Kin came aboard and were in the grow-decks before their bags were stowed, and the tired hydroponics answered their hands as if remembering something. The ship would eat greener for their coming.

ambient: Under the Keepers of the First Flame the founding was never far — the first-day rites kept in full, the old words spoken over small occasions, the ship steering by a faith in the ones who signed the charter.
A Keeper's quiet: the founders' log read aloud on the anniversaries, the charter honored to the letter, the ship holding its course by reverence for a hand two centuries cold.
name: Keepers of the First Flame
description: Austere traditionalists who guard the founders' first covenant against every revision. To the Keepers, change is only a slower kind of forgetting.
log_name: the Keepers of the First Flame

recruit_boon.flavor: The Keepers of the First Flame came aboard and lit their shrine-burn off the reactor before the first watch turned, and gave the ship a thing it had been missing: a fixed point, a fire that would not go out, a reason spoken aloud.
